outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Day 77: Hezbollah kills IDF soldier, fires rocket at Israel as Lebanon ceasefire fractures
outlets.summary: CNN tracks the Hezbollah ceasefire violations on Day 77 — a Hezbollah mortar killed Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan, 20, and a rocket was fired at Lower Galilee. CNN also covers the aftermath of the Beijing summit, with Trump claiming Xi pledged not to arm Iran, though analysts note Xi simultaneously confirmed China will keep buying Iranian oil.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Lawmakers question Hegseth over Iran war cost as Pentagon admits $29 billion price tag
outlets.summary: NPR covers bipartisan congressional grilling of Hegseth over the $29B war cost (up $4B from earlier estimate), shrinking munitions stockpile, and contradictions between public claims and classified intelligence on Iran's retained military capacity.

outlets.name: The Washington Post
outlets.headline: Hegseth, Caine face intense bipartisan frustration over Iran war conduct and costs
outlets.summary: WaPo analysis: Trump's claim that Iran has 'no Navy, no Air Force' contradicts classified intelligence showing Iran retained 70% of its pre-war missile arsenal and restored access to 30 of 33 missile sites. Hegseth's Senate testimony drew rare bipartisan criticism over the war's cost and unclear exit strategy.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump says Xi pledged no military arms to Iran; Rubio: both agree Hormuz cannot be 'militarized'
outlets.summary: Fox News frames the Trump-Xi Beijing summit as a diplomatic win, with Xi pledging not to arm Iran and both sides agreeing Hormuz must remain open. Frames Trump's 'win it one way or the other' remark as a show of strength in negotiations with Tehran.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: LIVE UPDATES: Trump Declares Iran 'Under Control' As He Vows To 'Finish The Job'
outlets.summary: Daily Wire coverage emphasizes Trump's confidence that Iran is 'under control,' his vow to 'finish the job,' and ongoing protests inside Iran demanding an end to Supreme Leader rule.

outlets.name: Newsmax
outlets.headline: 3 GOP Senators Support Dems in Failed Iran War Powers Vote
outlets.summary: Newsmax reports the 50-49 war powers vote with Murkowski, Collins, and Paul breaking ranks. Frames outcome as a Republican victory despite record defection.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live Day 77: Trump's China visit shadowed by Tehran conflict as BRICS meet in New Delhi
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera Day 77 liveblog covers Araghchi's 'never bow to pressure' address at the BRICS foreign ministers' meeting, the Trump-Xi Hormuz accord, Hezbollah ceasefire violations in Lebanon, and Netanyahu's Jerusalem Day speech declaring Iran 'weaker than ever.' Notes BRICS failed to reach a unified condemnation due to Iran-UAE divisions.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Hormuz Oil Flows Creep Higher as More Supertankers Exit Strait
outlets.summary: Bloomberg reports a slight easing in the Hormuz blockade — ~4 supertankers carrying Iraqi crude exited since May 10, equivalent to ~2M bbl/day. Analysts warn the volume is 'so low it won't make much of a difference.' Bloomberg also covered the earlier tanker seizure near UAE as evidence US diplomatic efforts are stumbling.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: From Iran to trade, China summit produces few concrete wins for Trump
outlets.summary: International analysis: Beijing and Washington aligned on Hormuz freedom of navigation and Iran's nuclear non-proliferation, but China simultaneously confirmed it will keep buying Iranian oil. Analysts say Xi gave symbolic support without meaningful leverage on Iran.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF soldier killed by Hezbollah mortar; IDF issues evacuation warning for south Lebanon villages ahead of strikes
outlets.summary: Times of Israel Day 77 liveblog leads with the death of Staff Sgt. Negev Dagan — the 6th IDF soldier killed in Lebanon since the ceasefire and 19th since the escalation. IDF issued an urgent evacuation warning for villages near Tyre and says rocket fire is a 'blatant violation' of ceasefire. Also tracks Netanyahu's Jerusalem Day declaration that 'Iran is weaker than ever.'

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IDF weighs return to Gaza fighting pending Iran ceasefire; UAE worked with Mossad during Iran war
outlets.summary: JPost reports IDF discussing potential Gaza reentry once Iran situation stabilizes. Reveals UAE intelligence cooperation with Mossad throughout the Iran war.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Netanyahu Secretly Visited UAE, Met President During Iran War, PM's Office Says
outlets.summary: Haaretz liveblog confirms Netanyahu UAE visit while also tracking Hezbollah explosive drone attacks against northern Israel. Notes Iran's parliament threatening 90% uranium enrichment.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: 'We will not give up the Strait of Hormuz at any cost': VP Aref issues defiant declaration
outlets.summary: PressTV leads with VP Aref's strong Hormuz declaration, framing it as Iran asserting its sovereign rights in the face of US-Chinese diplomatic pressure. Coverage positions Hormuz as an Iranian resource that cannot be surrendered regardless of international demands.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Netanyahu says Iran regime change 'possible, not guaranteed'; Iran calls it warmongering
outlets.summary: Iran International covers Netanyahu's CBS 60 Minutes admission that regime change is 'possible, not guaranteed,' framing it as revealing Israeli strategic intent. Also reports Iran's official response calling US-Israel actions 'expansionism and warmongering.'
